findings.message: Layering violation: the create_item route handler opens its own DB session via SessionLocal and manipulates the ORM directly (Item(...), session.add, session.refresh), bypassing both the get_session dependency used everywhere else in this file and the repository layer. db/repository.py already provides a create_item(session, name) function that performs this write correctly (including session.commit()). The route should depend on get_session and delegate to the repository rather than reaching into db.engine and the ORM itself.
findings.suggestion: Use the session dependency and the repository: `def create_item(payload: ItemCreate, session: SessionDep) -> Item: return repository.create_item(session, payload.name)`, and drop the `SessionLocal` import from db.engine.
